commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Google Vertex request URL builder to strip a redundant `google/` provider prefix from model IDs and adds a regression test for the constructed Vertex URL.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +40. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's Vertex URL builder percent-encodes `google/` in a provider-qualified model id, while the sibling Gemini builder strips the prefix first and Google's Vertex contract expects publisher and model as separate path segments.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer validation/override, not a code repair that ClawSweeper should automate.

target.title: fix(google): strip provider prefix from Vertex model path
target.body:
## Summary

`buildGoogleVertexRequestUrl` (`extensions/google/transport-stream.ts`) embeds `model.id` into the Vertex request URL with only URL-encoding. A provider-qualified id such as `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` therefore produces an invalid path segment:

`.../publishers/google/models/google%2Fgemini-3.1-pro-preview:streamGenerateContent` → Vertex 404.

The sibling Generative-AI path, `resolveGoogleModelPath`, already strips this prefix via `stripGoogleProviderPrefix`. This mirrors that on the Vertex path (one line; the helper is already imported).

This is the Vertex companion to #91125, which added the same strip to the Generative-AI path. Both URL builders share the invariant "the `google/` provider prefix must not reach the model path"; #91125 fixed one side, this fixes the twin.

## Verification

- Added a regression test mirroring the existing Vertex stream test, asserting the built URL strips the prefix.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts` → 64 passed (incl. the new test).
- `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` clean on the changed files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Google Vertex requests for a `google/`-prefixed model id built an invalid `publishers/google/models/google%2F...` URL (404); the prefix is now stripped, matching the Generative-AI path.
Real environment tested: local repo checkout (Node 24, pnpm 11.2.2); unit test via the repo's vitest runner.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: `Test Files  1 passed (1)` / `Tests  64 passed (64)`; the new test asserts the URL contains `/publishers/google/models/gemini-3.1-pro-preview:streamGenerateContent` and not `google%2F`.
Observed result after fix: the Vertex URL no longer contains the `google%2F` segment; the model path matches Generative-AI path behavior.
What was not tested: a live Google Vertex API round-trip (no GCP credentials available). The change is pure URL construction, verified by unit test and by symmetry with the merged #91125 fix.
target.author: bymle
